Question Text: Chemicals secreted by soil fungi which inhibit the growth of bacteria are known as ___.
Which of the following is an important role for fungi in the carbon cycle?
Option 1: Fungi release fixed carbon back to the environment for other plants and photosynthetic organisms to utilize.
Option 2: Fungi provide fixed carbon to plants for the production of plant cellular tissues.
Option 3: Fungi fix carbon by undergoing photosynthesis.

Question Text: When pathogenic fungi are found growing on the roots of grape vines grape farmers sometimes respond by covering the ground around their vines with plastic sheeting and pumping a gaseous fungicide into the soil. The most important concern of grape farmers who engage in this practice should be that the ___.
Option 1: fungicide might also kill the native yeasts residing on the surfaces of the grapes
Option 2: lichens growing on the vines' branches are not harmed
Option 4: sheeting is transparent so that photosynthesis can continue

Question Text: Fungi that absorb nutrients from decaying plant matter are called ___.
All fungi are ___.
Which of the following is major characteristic of fungi that distinguishes them from other eukaryotes?
Why is it more difficult to treat fungal infections than bacterial infections in humans?
Option 1: Fungi are larger organisms than bacteria and thus require stronger drugs to stop an infection.
Option 2: Most fungi are multicellular and thus the drugs required to treat a fungal infection must be able to kill several types of cells; bacteria ; on the other hand ; are unicellular and thus simpler to kill.
Option 3: Fungal and animal cells and proteins are similar. Thus ; drugs that disrupt fungal cell or protein function may also disrupt human cell or protein function.
Option 4: Fungi are able to mutate more quickly than bacteria ; so they quickly develop resistance to antifungal drugs.

Question Text: Long branching fungal filaments are called ___.
The vegetative (nutritionally active) bodies of most fungi are ___.

You are a forester charged with increasing productivity in a South American forest newly planted with pines from Oregon. You believe that the southern forest lacks the fungal diversity needed by the North American pines and that this lack of fungi is affecting the pines' productivity ; but you have no evidence to support your ideas. Which of the following would be the best approach to assess the diversity of fungi likely contributing to tree productivity in the Oregon forest?
Option 1: Count the number of fungal species visible on the leaves of one tree species and then multiply by the number of trees in the forest.
Option 2: Collect all the fruiting structures (mushrooms ; morels ; etc.) found aboveground.
Option 3: Do direct sequencing on representative soil samples from across the forest.
Option 4: Expose the trees to radiolabeled phosphorus and then collect the soil samples with the greatest radioactivity and do direct sequencing.

Question Text: Which of these fungal features supports the phylogenetic conclusion that fungi are more closely related to animals than to plants?
Option 1: Fungi are able to change their body shape continuously throughout their life.
Option 4: The cell walls of fungi are made of chitin.

Question Text: The question(s) below refer to the following phylogenetic trees. Which tree depicts the microsporidians as a sister group of the ascomycetes?
Microsporidians were long thought to be a sister group to fungi. Which of the phylogenies best supports this view?
Among the organisms listed here which are thought to be the closest relatives of fungi?
Studies have shown that in some forest ecosystems carbon can move from one tree to another via mycorrhizal fungi. Which of the following features of fungal biology enables this?
Option 1: The cells of fungal hyphae are connected by pores that allow passage of cytoplasm from cell to cell.
Option 2: Fungal cells have large numbers of transporter proteins for moving digested materials around the mycelia.
Option 3: Fungal cells have flagella which can help with movement of materials.
Option 4: Mycorrhizae form symbiotic associations with bacteria that assist with transport.

Question Text: It has been hypothesized that fungi and plants have a mutualistic relationship because plants make sugars available for the fungi's use. What is the best evidence in support of this hypothesis?
Option 1: Fungi survive better when they are associated with plants.
Option 2: Radioactively labeled sugars produced by plants eventually show up in the fungi with which they are associated.
Option 3: Fungi associated with plants have the ability to undergo photosynthesis and produce their own sugars ; whereas those not associated with plants do not produce their own sugars.
Option 4: Radioactive labeling experiments show that plants pass crucial raw materials to the fungus for manufacturing sugars.

Question Text: You have discovered a new species of fungi. Which of the following features would support describing this new species as a zygomycete?
Option 1: The fungus forms yoked hyphae during reproduction.
Option 3: The fungus has a multicellular n phase as part of its life cycle.
Option 4: The fungus produces sporangia containing exactly 8 spores.
Option 5: The fungus is able to form associations with plant roots.

Question Text: At which stage of a basidiomycete's life cycle would reproduction be halted if an enzyme that prevented the fusion of hyphae was introduced?
When a mycelium infiltrates an unexploited source of dead organic matter ; what are most likely to appear within the food source soon thereafter?
Fungi with hyphae ___.
Option 3: have cell walls that consist mainly of cellulose microfibrils
Option 4: are adapted for rapid directional growth to new food sources
Option 5: reproduce asexually by a process known as budding

Question Text: In septate fungi ; what structures allow cytoplasmic streaming to distribute needed nutrients ; synthesized compounds ; and organelles throughout the hyphae?
Option 5: Tight junctions that form in cross-walls between cells

Question Text: Which of the following characteristics is unique to chytrids compared to other groups of fungi?
Option 4: Presence of a multicellular haploid stage

Question Text: You are given an organism to identify. It has a fruiting body that contains many structures with eight haploid spores lined up in a row. What kind of a fungus is this?
Apart from direct amphibian-to-amphibian contact ; what is the most likely means by which the zoospores spread from one free-living amphibian to another?
The adaptive advantage associated with the filamentous nature of fungal mycelia is primarily related to ___.
Option 1: the ability to parasitize other organisms
Option 2: avoiding sexual reproduction until the environment changes
Option 3: the potential to inhabit almost all terrestrial habitats
Option 4: the increased probability of contact between different mating types
Option 5: an extensive surface area well suited for absorptive nutrition

Question Text: It has been hypothesized that fungi and plants have a mutualistic relationship because fungi provide critical nitrogen for the plants' use. How do we know this happens? In experiments using radioactively labeled ___.
Option 1: nitrogen ; plants acquired more radioactive nitrogen when they were associated with fungi
Option 2: nitrogen ; labeled nitrogen showed up in fungi when the fungi were symbiotic with plants
Option 3: carbon ; plants acquired more radioactive carbon when they were associated with fungi

Question Text: Why are mycorrhizal fungi superior to plants at acquiring mineral nutrition from the soil?
Option 1: Hyphae are 100 to 1000 times larger than plant roots.
Option 2: Hyphae have a smaller surface-area-to-volume ratio than do the hairs on a plant root.
Option 3: Mycelia are able to grow in the direction of food.
Option 4: Fungi secrete extracellular enzymes that can break down large molecules.

Question Text: Basidiomycetes are the only fungal group capable of synthesizing lignin peroxidase. What advantage does this group of fungi have over other fungi because of this capability?
Option 1: This is always the first group of fungi to begin any kind of plant decomposition.
Option 2: This fungal group can break down the tough lignin ; which cannot be harnessed for energy ; to gain access to the more useful energy source cellulose.
Option 3: This is the only group of fungi that can use lignin for adenosine triphosphate (ATP) production.
Option 4: This enzyme releases heat energy from the breakdown of lignin that is used to kill off competing fungi.

Question Text: Why is it important that ectomycorrhizal fungi (EMF) have peptidase enzymes?
Option 1: These enzymes are necessary to break through the tough lignin layers in plants.
Option 2: These enzymes assist with the breakdown of cellulose.
Option 3: These enzymes are needed to release nitrogen from dead plant material.

Question Text: What group of fungi has the ability to penetrate its host's cell wall ; thus increasing the efficiency with which materials are passed from fungus to host?
Some fungal species can kill herbivores while feeding off of sugars from its plant host. What type of relationship does this fungus have with its host?
Based on the graphs in the accompanying figure which of the following plant species is most likely NOT to form mycorrhizal associations?

Based on graphs in the accompanying figure ; which of the following is the best description of the data supporting the idea that a plant species did NOT form mycorrhizae with a fungus? Its biomass is the greatest when ___.
In graph (b) in the accompanying figure which of the following best explains the data given about Lotus corniculatus?
Option 1: This plant grows best when AMF taxa A is present.
Option 2: Lotus corniculatus does not form mycorrhizal associations.
Option 3: Mycorrhizal fungi parasitize the plant's roots when they are present reducing its growth.
Option 4: This plant forms multiple AMF associations growing best with increased fungal diversity.

Question Text: What is the major difference between Bromus erectus (graph f) and the other plant species (graphs a-d) included in the study?
Option 1: Bromus erectus grows best with a diversity of fungal partners.
Option 2: Bromus erectus is unaffected by AMF diversity.
Option 3: Bromus erectus shows dramatic reduction in growth in the presence of AMF.
Option 4: Bromus erectus produces very little biomass regardless of AMF.

Question Text: Why does total biomass (graph e in the accompanying figure) NOT vary with AMF diversity?
Option 1: Plant growth is unaffected by fungal diversity.
Option 2: Most of the plant species in this system do not form mycorrhizal associations.
Option 3: Lotus corniculatus the dominant plant species.

Question Text: Which of the following terms refers to symbiotic relationships involving fungi living between the cells in plant leaves?
In most fungi ; karyogamy does not immediately follow plasmogamy ; which consequently ___.
Option 1: means that sexual reproduction can occur in specialized structures
Option 2: results in multiple diploid nuclei per cell
Option 3: allows fungi to reproduce asexually most of the time
Option 4: results in heterokaryotic or potentially even dikaryotic cells
Option 5: is strong support for the claim that fungi are not truly eukaryotic

Question Text: [Text Missing] resemble animal sperm. To which of the following groups does the fungus belong?

Question Text: In both lichens and mycorrhizae ; what does the fungal partner provide to its photosynthetic partner?
Microsporidians are considered parasitic because of the ability to penetrate their host cells using ___.
Many amphibian populations have been decimated by a parasitic fungus classified as a member of the ___.
Lichens are symbiotic associations of fungi and ___.
Which of the following best describes the physical relationship of the partners involved in lichens?
Option 1: Fungal cells are enclosed within algal cells.
Option 2: Lichen cells are enclosed within fungal cells.
Option 3: Photosynthetic cells are surrounded by fungal hyphae.

Question Text: ___ form ectomycorrhizal relationships with temperate forest trees while ___ form arbuscular (endomycorrhizal) relationships with plants in grasslands and tropical forests.
Some fungi have been instrumental in the development of human culture including ___ in the phylum ___ as they have been in use for hundreds of years in producing beer and bread.
Which of the following fungal groups may NOT be monophyletic?

Our current phylogeny of the fungi contains a polytomy. If additional data allowed us to resolve the polytomy ; and we discovered that Zygomycetes are monophyletic ; but Chytrids are not monophyletic ; which of the following hypotheses about fungal evolution would most likely be correct?
Option 1: Flagellated spores may have evolved several times.
Option 2: The zygosporangium may have evolved several times.

Question Text: A fungal species produces spores in sets of four that are mounted on a short stalk. Which of the following features would you NOT expect to find in this species?

Upon visiting Malheur National Forest ; this "largest living organism" won't be obvious to you ; because the vast majority is found underground. The underground portion of the fungus is referred to as the ___.
The reproductive structure of Armillaria ostoyae forms aboveground and has the stereotypical mushroom shape. Therefore ; A. ostoyae represents a fungus from which key lineage?

Option 2: divide by mitosis to make sexual fruiting bodies

Question Text: In fungal life cycles plasmogamy results in the formation of ___.
Option 1: dikaryotic hyphae that form the reproductive structure
Option 2: a diploid nucleus that will undergo meiosis
Option 3: diploid hyphae that form the reproductive structure

Question Text: Despite the misconception that plants and fungi are closely related fungi are actually more closely related to animals. Which of the following is a correct statement that supports this relationship?
Option 1: Both fungi and animals are always multicellular.
Option 2: Both fungi and animals have mitochondria.
Option 3: Both fungi and animals have cell walls made of chitin
Option 4: Both fungi and animals are heterotrophic.

Question Text: You are looking at slides of plant root tissue for a research project ; and notice some cells that appear to have branching darkly stained structures inside of the root cells. You remember learning about plant mutualisms with fungi and determine these structures are most likely due to ___ from the fungal lineage ___.
Basidiomycota is distinguished from the ascomycota because members of the basidiomycota ___.
Option 1: produce 4 spores at the end of a round of sexual reproduction
Option 2: have many members that reproduce exclusively asexually using conidia
Option 3: undergo karyogamy in a club shaped structure that will contain the sexual spores
Option 4: produce reproductive structures composed of dikaryotic hyphae

Question Text: Conidia are ___.
Option 1: structures involved in asexual reproduction in all fungi
Option 2: structures involved in sexual reproduction in some groups of fungi
Option 3: structures involved in asexual reproduction in some groups of fungi
Option 4: structures involved in asexual reproduction in some groups of fungi
